text.title: accessibility-first-design Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for accessibility-first-design. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Enforce a minimum contrast ratio of 4.5:1 for body text and 3:1 for large text (18pt+), UI components, and focus indicators; embed these thresholds in design tokens so non-compliant primitives cannot be exported.
rule.rationale: WCAG 2.2 SC 1.4.3 and 1.4.11 define these ratios. Encoding them in tokens prevents a designer from accidentally choosing a brand color that fails at the theme level before any component is built.

rule.statement: Never use color as the sole indicator of meaning; pair every color signal with a shape, label, or pattern.
rule.rationale: Approximately 8% of males have color vision deficiency. Color-only signals (red = error) are invisible to this population and fail WCAG SC 1.4.1.

rule.statement: Set minimum interactive element size to 44x44 CSS pixels (or platform equivalent); ensure sufficient spacing between adjacent targets so misfire probability is low.
rule.rationale: WCAG 2.2 SC 2.5.8 (Target Size, Minimum) formalizes this. Spacing matters independently of size — clustered 44px targets still cause misfires for users with motor impairments or tremor.

rule.statement: Make focus indicators visible with at least 3:1 contrast against the adjacent background; never rely on browser defaults which often fail this threshold.
rule.rationale: WCAG 2.2 SC 2.4.11 (Focus Appearance, Minimum) requires a visible focus ring. Browser defaults (thin dotted outline) are often invisible on colored backgrounds.

rule.statement: Specify heading level (H1-H6), landmark roles (nav, main, header, footer), and ARIA labels in design handoff annotations; do not leave semantic decisions to developer interpretation.
rule.rationale: Developers recreate heading hierarchies inconsistently without explicit annotation. Screen reader users navigate by headings; a flat or misordered hierarchy breaks the primary navigation pattern.

rule.statement: Include pause/stop controls for all auto-playing animations longer than 5 seconds; respect prefers-reduced-motion at the CSS level.
rule.rationale: WCAG 2.2 SC 2.2.2 (Pause, Stop, Hide) and 2.3.3 (Animation from Interactions) protect users with vestibular disorders and cognitive disabilities from motion that prevents reading or causes nausea.

rule.statement: Never apply automated contrast or target-size fixes without designer review; fixes often break visual hierarchy (e.g., darkening a brand color to pass AA).
rule.rationale: Automated fixes optimize for the accessibility metric, not for design intent. A darkened brand primary may pass contrast but invalidate the brand. Fixes must be flagged for designer decision, not silently applied.

rule.statement: Track accessibility issues found at design stage versus handoff stage versus production; the ratio of design-stage finds to later-stage finds is the shift-left KPI.
rule.rationale: If most finds are in production rather than design, the design-phase process is not working. The KPI makes the shift-left investment visible and improvable.
